channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SpaceX tourists return to Earth after a private trip that orbited the poles
item.description: Four space tourists who orbited the north and south poles returned to Earth on Friday, splashing down in the Pacific to end their privately funded polar tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DARPA backs multiple quantum paths in benchmarking initiative
item.description: The Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency has selected 15 quantum computing companies for its Quantum Benchmarking Initiative, to see if utility-scale quantum computers can be built by 2033.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NetBox Labs embraces intersection of network management and AI
item.description: Over the last several years, NetBox has established itself as a widely used open-source tool for understanding and modeling networks. NetBox Labs, the lead commercial sponsor behind the open-source project, has continued to push ahead and expand beyond just the core NetBox project.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk, Navarro tangle on tariffs
item.description: Tech billionaire and close Trump ally Elon Musk tangled with the president's trade adviser Peter Navarro on tariffs over the weekend, as the administration’s new import taxes have roiled the stock market.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Keen to buy an RTX 5060 Ti? Benchmarks and more rumored sightings suggest you don’t have long to wait for Nvidia’s next GPU
item.description: Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti benchmarks appear, alongside sightings of Asus GPUs, all suggesting the launch rumored for next week is going ahead.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Boosting Efficiency and Productivity with ITSM Automation
item.description: IT Service Management (ITSM) automation can empower organizations to achieve superior efficiency, accuracy, and scalability while improving employee morale and productivity.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google NotebookLM cheat sheet: Get fast insights into your docs
item.description: When Google first launched the NotebookLM beta in late 2023, the company billed it as an experimental note-taking app with a generative AI assistant built in. You can still use it to create notes, but the app has evolved into something much more novel and powerful.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra could have a new camera lens but no magnets
item.description: The latest Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra rumor points to minimal changes to the main camera, and no changes to two other features.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: There may be a huge catch to buying physical Nintendo Switch 2 Edition games
item.description: Physical Nintendo Switch 2 Edition games might just be original Switch cartridges with a download code for the upgrade.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: GISEC 2025: Empowering cybersecurity and Women in Tech
item.description: GISEC Global 2025, set to take place in Dubai, promises to be a pivotal event in the cybersecurity landscape, offering a platform for innovation, networking, and knowledge sharing.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump administration rolls back forest protections in bid to ramp up logging
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has rolled back environmental protections around future logging projects on more than half of U.S. national forests under an emergency designation issued Friday that cites the dangers of wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly half of National Weather Service offices have 20% vacancy rates, and experts say it's a risk
item.description: Data obtained by The Associated Press shows that close to half of the local National Weather Service forecast offices have 20% vacancy rates as severe weather chugs across the nation’s heartland.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates reflects upon a 50-year-old computer code that reshaped technology
item.description: Even as he grows older, Microsoft founder Bill Gates still fondly remembers the catalytic computer code he wrote 50 years ago that opened up a new frontier in technology.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tariff war throws building of data centers into disarray
item.description: Enterprise IT leaders are facing a double-whammy of uncertainties complicating their data center building decisions: The ever-changing realities of genAI strategies, and the back-and-forth nature of the current tariff wars pushed by the United States. “This is obviously a fluid situation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ServiceNow to acquire Logik.ai to boost CRM portfolio
item.description: ServiceNow is acquiring Logik.ai and its configure, price, quote (CPQ) solution to expand its customer relationship management (CRM) product portfolio and accelerate its momentum in sales and order management. Logik.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mechanical Orchard taps gen AI for mainframe-to-cloud modernization
item.description: IBM is expected to drive AI deep into its next mainframe refresh, due this spring, in a move aimed at keeping and attracting Big Iron customers. Meanwhile, there’s no shortage of vendors vying for enterprise customers that are interested in moving off the mainframe and into the cloud.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Accelerate cloud infrastructure initiatives with Lucid Software
item.description: Cloud and development operations (devops) teams are under constant pressure to do more, faster. They have outdated systems to update and manage, new systems to build, and requests from business units who need their support. It’s a velocity problem, and IT departments often struggle to keep pace.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge blocks social media age-verification law in Arkansas
item.description: The Arkansas law would require people under 18 to only access certain sites with parental permission.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: IBM Cloud speeds AI workloads with Intel Gaudi 3 accelerators
item.description: IBM Cloud is broadening its AI technology services with Intel Gaudi 3 AI accelerators now available to enterprise customers.
item.dc:creator: Network World
